votes.thesis: Dollar and rates pressure dominate the near-term FX balance.
Oil shock transmission is the main imported stress channel for INR.
India-specific tone and panic skew the market toward local stress pricing.
Global risk balance and safe-haven demand drive the short-horizon move.
Trend pressure and regime persistence favor continuation.
The move looks stretched enough that reversal risk matters.
Carry and relative stability shape how sticky the move can be.
Tail-risk management demands extra caution under stress and event heat.
